$schema_note: Negation particles and the effect each has on the GLOSS — tense, mood, and meaning. A hover gloss must follow the governing negative, not the bare surface tense of the verb. لَمْ over a present-tense FORM yields a PAST meaning; لَنْ negates the future; لَا/مَا depend on context. Consume alongside particle-context-rules.json (homograph harakāt) — لَمْ vs لِمَ is decided there first, then the negation effect here.
generator: authored from the verb-chart negation columns (لن/لم) + standard nahw; field names aligned to nahw/examples/function-word-decisions.jsonl

rules.effect: negates the PAST. The verb is present-tense in FORM but past in MEANING.
rules.example: لَمْ يَلِدْ وَلَمْ يُولَدْ → 'He did not beget, nor was He begotten'
rules.guard: distinct from the interrogative لِمَ 'why' (kasra on lām) — resolve the homograph via particle-context-rules.json#lam_vs_lima FIRST.

rules.example: لَنْ تَنَالُوا الْبِرَّ → 'You will never attain righteousness'

rules.effect: multi-function: (a) لا النافية — simple negation 'does not'; (b) لا الناهية — prohibition + jussive 'do not!'; (c) لا النافية للجنس — absolute negation 'there is no …'; (d) bare 'no'.
rules.example: لَا إِلَٰهَ إِلَّا اللَّهُ → 'there is no god but Allah' (لا of absolute negation)
rules.guard: the prohibitive لا (+ jussive verb) vs the negating لا (+ indicative) need the following verb's mood; if the next token's POS/mood is unknown → pending (see nahw SKILL §'Do not resolve').

rules.effect: as a negative: negates a past verb ('did not') or a nominal predicate ('is not'). But مَا is ALSO relative / interrogative / maṣdariyyah — negation is only one reading.
rules.example: مَا قَتَلُوهُ → 'they did not kill him'
rules.guard: do NOT default مَا to negation; without a clear negation/relative/interrogative signal → pending. See particle decision ladder.

rules.governs: nominal sentence (raises the noun, accusatives the predicate)
rules.effect: defective verb of negation — 'is not / are not' (present-state negation of a nominal sentence).
rules.example: لَيْسَ كَمِثْلِهِ شَيْءٌ → 'there is nothing like Him'

standing_order: Resolve the homograph (harakāt) first, then apply the negation effect. If the governed verb's mood/POS is needed but unknown, prefer pending(negation_scope) over a wrong tense gloss. The negative's scope, not the verb's surface tense, sets the English.
